multiple_choice.question: I don't mind _____ late to finish the community service project.
_____ the security cameras _____ the guards help keep this neighborhood secure.
The victim was vulnerable because she let _____ guard down in the crowded area.
_____ pickpockets _____ purse-snatchers are common in tourist attractions.
What really _____ me off is when people cut in line without apologizing.
The residents were annoyed by _____ loudly in the hallway late at night.
_____ the bustling city life _____ the slower pace of rural living appeals to different people.
You should keep your valuables in _____ pockets to avoid theft.
_____ the crowded subway _____ the noisy streets bother him now that he is accustomed to urban life.
I appreciate _____ so courteous to the out-of-town visitors.
_____ we choose to live in a suburb _____ in a densely-populated area, safety should be our priority.
What really gets on my _____ is when people honk their car horns late at night.
The mugger targeted the tourist because of _____ around with his phone without paying attention.
_____ the beautiful amenities _____ the well-maintained historical buildings make this ideal neighborhood attractive.
Residents should stay _____ of their surroundings, especially in high-crime neighborhoods.
The crime prevention workshop emphasized _____ your guard down in unfamiliar areas.
_____ violent crime _____ property theft have decreased since more security cameras were installed.
It goes without _____ that you should avoid walking alone in poorly lit areas.
_____ can spoil your experience in an otherwise beautiful city.
The police officer insisted on _____ their identification before letting them enter.
People who live in this area are used to _____ their own business and not intruding on others.
_____ did the criminals target tourists, _____ they operated near popular attractions.
The community's _____ the park helped reduce litter and criminal activities.
_____ you live in a bustling urban area _____ a quiet rural one, you should always look out for your neighbors.
There's _____ in numbers, so try to travel with friends when visiting unfamiliar places.
The community leaders were happy about _____ so much money for local charities.
_____ the fast-paced lifestyle _____ the constant noise are things you have to accept in the city center.
What really bugs me is when people smoke _____ near public buildings.
The success of the Ice Bucket Challenge was due to _____ attention to a meaningful cause.
_____ you settle in the suburbs _____ in the city, it will take some getting used to.

gap_fill.question: Living in a bustling city can be exciting, but it will ____________________ (take / get / use) for someone from a rural area.
____________________ (both / security cameras / and / guards) help reduce criminal activities in this urban area.
I really appreciate ____________________ (neighbors / look out) for each other during the holiday season.
You should always walk in ____________________ (well / light) areas to avoid becoming a target for criminals.
____________________ (either / you / settle in) a densely-populated neighborhood or a quiet suburb, you will find both have advantages and disadvantages.
The victim didn't notice ____________________ (pickpocket / take) her wallet because she was distracted.
What gets on my nerves is ____________________ (people / cut) in line without any consideration for others.
The new residents are slowly ____________________ (settle) into the community and joining local activities.
____________________ (not only / theft) but also violent crime have decreased in this area since the new measures were implemented.
Even though some neighbors may seem nosy, they usually ____________________ (mean) well.
The ____________________ (amenities) in this ideal neighborhood include a gym, a swimming pool, and a community garden.
Tourists are often ____________________ (vulnerable) to purse-snatchers because they are distracted by their surroundings.
The success of the fundraising event was due to ____________________ (everyone / donate) generously.
When moving to a new city, it's important to ____________________ (look / bright side) and focus on the opportunities.
____________________ (neither / the noise) nor the crowded streets bother her now that she is accustomed to urban life.
The residents were concerned about ____________________ (strangers / congregate) outside their building late at night.
I don't mind ____________________ (neighbors / mind) their own business, but I appreciate when they offer help when needed.
____________________ (both / high-rise apartment buildings) and well-maintained historical buildings can be found in this area.
It's always ____________________ (better / safe / sorry), so keep your valuables in a secure place.
____________________ (all / all), this neighborhood has a lot to offer despite its minor drawbacks.
